questions.question: What is the primary function of a page table in an operating system?
options: A) To manage memory allocation
B) To translate virtual addresses to physical addresses
C) To manage file systems
D) To manage user accounts
questions.explanation: A page table is used to translate virtual addresses into physical addresses, allowing the operating system to manage memory efficiently.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Page Directory in a page table?
options: A) To store page table entries
B) To store kernel data
C) To store memory mapping information
D) To store process information
questions.explanation: The Page Directory is a data structure that stores page table entries, which provide a mapping between virtual page numbers and physical frame numbers.

questions.question: What happens when a process requests a page that is not in physical memory?
options: A) The operating system allocates a new physical frame
B) The operating system swaps the page out to disk
C) The operating system blocks the process until the page is available
D) The operating system maps the page from disk to physical memory
questions.explanation: When a process requests a page that is not in physical memory, the operating system may swap the page out to disk (also known as a page fault) or allocate a new physical frame to hold the page.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Page Table Entry (PTE) in a page table?
options: A) To store the process ID of the process
B) To store the page number in physical memory
C) To store the page number and physical frame number in physical memory
D) To store the file permissions of the file
questions.explanation: A Page Table Entry (PTE) is a data structure that stores the page number and physical frame number in physical memory, providing a mapping between virtual page numbers and physical frame numbers.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Page Fault Handler in a page table?
options: A) To manage memory allocation
B) To translate virtual addresses to physical addresses
C) To handle page faults and page outages
D) To manage file systems
questions.explanation: The Page Fault Handler is a mechanism that handles page faults, which occur when a process requests a page that is not in physical memory. It can allocate a new physical frame, swap the page out to disk, or block the process until the page is available.
